prompt: Create an interactive scrollytelling visualization like the Preston Curve demo, but with three states: (1) a world map showing GDP per capita, (2) scatter plot of GDP vs life expectancy, (3) a stacked bar chart showing regional breakdowns. Include smooth SVG path morphing between states using flubber, and add labels that fade in/out appropriately. User should scroll to trigger transitions.

assertions.text: HTML file generated and contains valid SVG elements
assertions.evidence: scrollytelling-visualization.html exists and contains <svg> tag with proper structure

assertions.text: All three visualization states (map, scatter, bars) are implemented
assertions.evidence: Code includes state functions for map grid, scatter plot, and stacked bars with proper data binding

assertions.text: Uses Flubber for smooth path morphing between states
assertions.evidence: Flubber library loaded from CDN and attrTween used for smooth transitions

assertions.text: IntersectionObserver used for scroll-triggered state changes
assertions.evidence: IntersectionObserver configured with rootMargin and triggers state functions on scroll

assertions.text: All shapes in same coordinate space (no transform tricks that break morphing)
assertions.evidence: Paths generated in consistent SVG coordinate space, all transforms applied at group level

assertions.text: Labels fade in and out appropriately with state changes
assertions.evidence: Label opacity transitions implemented, visible only in map state
